req.title: Manager updates employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can update an employee via UI, with mandatory field validation, audit record creation, and appropriate HTTP status codes. Test cases cover: (1) EP positive path — valid full update by manager yields 200/201 and audit entry; (2) NEG negative path — submitting the form with a missing mandatory field yields a 400 and no update; (3) BVA boundary — employee first_name at maximum allowed length (255 chars) succeeds; (4) STC state transition — unauthenticated session triggers 401 redirect; (5) DT decision table — forbidden role (viewer) triggers 403. Together these cover all stated error pathways and success conditions.

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_valid_data
preconditions: A target employee record exists with known id
actions: Clear and fill all mandatory fields with valid data
Query audit log for the employee id
expected: Response body contains the updated employee entity id
Employee record in the system reflects the new values
Audit log contains exactly one new record for this update action
trace: R-WEB-002: response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_updates_employee_valid_data(manager_page, existing_employee, audit_log_client):
    emp_id = existing_employee["id"]
    page = manager_page

    page.goto(f"/employees/{emp_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='first_name']", "Alice")
    page.fill("[data-testid='last_name']", "Smith")
    page.fill("[data-testid='email']", "alice.smith@example.com")
    page.fill("[data-testid='department']", "Engineering")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/employees/{emp_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-update']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(emp_id)

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1, "No audit record found for update"

tests.name: test_manager_update_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400
preconditions: A target employee record exists with known id
actions: Clear the mandatory 'last_name' field, leave it empty
Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
expected: UI displays a validation error message for last_name
Employee record is NOT updated in the system
No new audit record is written
trace: R-WEB-002: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_update_employee_missing_mandatory_field_returns_400(manager_page, existing_employee, audit_log_client):
    emp_id = existing_employee["id"]
    original_last_name = existing_employee["last_name"]
    page = manager_page

    page.goto(f"/employees/{emp_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='first_name']", "Alice")
    page.fill("[data-testid='last_name']", "")  # intentionally blank
    page.fill("[data-testid='email']", "alice.smith@example.com")
    page.fill("[data-testid='department']", "Engineering")

    pre_audit_count = len(audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE"))

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/employees/{emp_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-update']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {response.status}"

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='error-last_name']")).to_be_visible()

    post_audit_count = len(audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert post_audit_count == pre_audit_count, "Audit record must NOT be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_manager_update_employee_first_name_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: A target employee record exists with known id
System allows first_name up to 255 characters
actions: Fill all other mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Employee record is updated with the 255-char first_name
trace: R-WEB-002: mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_manager_update_employee_first_name_at_max_boundary_length(manager_page, existing_employee, audit_log_client):
    emp_id = existing_employee["id"]
    page = manager_page
    max_name = "A" * 255

    page.goto(f"/employees/{emp_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='first_name']", max_name)
    page.fill("[data-testid='last_name']", "Smith")
    page.fill("[data-testid='email']", "boundary@example.com")
    page.fill("[data-testid='department']", "QA")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/employees/{emp_id}" in r.url and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-update']")

    response = resp_info.value
    assert response.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201 for max-length name, got {response.status}"

    body = response.json()
    assert body.get("first_name") == max_name, "first_name not persisted at max length"

    audit_records = audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_records) >= 1

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_update_employee_returns_401
preconditions: User is NOT authenticated (no valid session cookie or token)
A target employee record exists with known id
actions: Attempt to navigate to /employees/{id}/edit without authenticating
expected: User is redirected to the login page or receives an unauthorised message
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import sync_playwright

def test_unauthenticated_update_employee_returns_401(base_url, existing_employee):
    emp_id = existing_employee["id"]

    with sync_playwright() as pw:
        browser = pw.chromium.launch()
        # Fresh context — no stored auth state
        context = browser.new_context()
        page = context.new_page()

        responses = []
        page.on("response", lambda r: responses.append(r) if f"/employees/{emp_id}" in r.url else None)

        page.goto(f"{base_url}/employees/{emp_id}/edit")
        page.wait_for_load_state("networkidle")

        status_codes = [r.status for r in responses]
        # Either a direct 401 or a redirect chain ending at login page
        is_401 = 401 in status_codes
        is_login_redirect = "login" in page.url or "signin" in page.url

        assert is_401 or is_login_redirect, (
            f"Expected 401 or login redirect, got statuses={status_codes} url={page.url}"
        )

        browser.close()

tests.name: test_forbidden_role_update_employee_returns_403
preconditions: User is authenticated with role=viewer (not manager)
A target employee record exists with known id
actions: Send a PUT/PATCH request to /employees/{id} with valid payload
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_forbidden_role_update_employee_returns_403(viewer_api_client, existing_employee, audit_log_client):
    emp_id = existing_employee["id"]
    payload = {
        "first_name": "Hacker",
        "last_name": "Attempt",
        "email": "hacker@example.com",
        "department": "Unauthorized"
    }

    pre_audit_count = len(audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE"))

    response = viewer_api_client.patch(f"/employees/{emp_id}", json=payload)

    assert response.status_code == 403, (
        f"Expected 403 for viewer role, got {response.status_code}"
    )

    body = response.json()
    assert any(
        kw in str(body).lower() for kw in ("forbidden", "not allowed", "permission", "role")
    ), f"Expected forbidden message in body, got: {body}"

    post_audit_count = len(audit_log_client.get_records(entity_id=emp_id, action="UPDATE"))
    assert post_audit_count == pre_audit_count, "Audit record must NOT be written on 403"
